perturbation.original_text: Such reimbursements shall be made by the Operating Company to EmployeeCo in advance or immediately upon such costs being incurred, or otherwise in accordance with historical practice, unless otherwise agreed between the Operating Company and EmployeeCo.
perturbation.changed_text: Such reimbursements shall be made by the Operating Company to EmployeeCo within 30 days of such costs being incurred, notwithstanding any historical practice, unless otherwise agreed between the Operating Company and EmployeeCo.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. Originally, reimbursements were to be made 'in advance or immediately upon such costs being incurred, or otherwise in accordance with historical practice.' Now, reimbursements must be made within 30 days of costs being incurred, regardless of prior practice. This contradicts the 'historical practice' clause. It is in direct contradiction to Section 1.4 where the parties may settle their financial obligations under this Agreement Pursuant to the Parties' normal inter-affiliate settlement processes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties may settle their financial obligations under this Agreement Pursuant to the Parties' normal inter-affiliate settlement processes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text mandates reimbursement within 30 days, explicitly overriding 'historical practice'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which allows for financial obligations to be settled according to the parties' normal inter-affiliate settlement processes, potentially encompassing a different timeframe or method than the 30-day rule. The change introduces a specific deadline that conflicts with the previously agreed-upon flexibility.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated by (a) the written agreement of the Parties or (b) by either Party upon 5 days written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated by (a) the written agreement of the Parties or (b) by EmployeeCo upon 5 days written notice to the other Party, or (c) by the Operating Company upon 90 days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by creating differing termination notice periods for each party. EmployeeCo can terminate with 5 days' notice, while the Operating Company requires 90 days' notice. This is in direct contradiction of the written agreement of the Parties of Section 2.3 where the agreement may be terminated by either Party upon 5 days written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated by (a) the written agreement of the Parties or (b) by either Party upon 5 days written notice to the other Party. All payment obligations hereunder shall survive the termination of this Agreement in accordance with their respective terms.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces different termination notice periods (5 days for EmployeeCo, 90 days for the Operating Company) while the contradicted text states 'either Party upon 5 days written notice'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the termination notice period, making it unclear which period applies to which party, and therefore unenforceable as written. The change breaks the original agreement of a uniform 5-day notice period for termination.

perturbation.original_text: No Party shall have the right to assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the consent of the other Parties hereto; provided, however, that either party hereto may make a collateral assignment of this Agreement solely to secure working capital financing for such party.
perturbation.changed_text: No Party shall have the right to assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement. Any assignments, subcontracts or any kind of transfers shall be considered to be void ab initio.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. The original text allowed for collateral assignment to secure working capital. The modified text completely prohibits any assignment, including collateral assignments, which contradicts the earlier allowance. This creates an in-text contradiction because previously assignments can be made if either party wants to secure working capital financing, creating uncertainty about the validity of such assignment. Contradiction of Section 2.5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Party shall have the right to assign its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the consent of the other Parties hereto; provided, however, that either party hereto may make a collateral assignment of this Agreement solely to secure working capital financing for such party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for collateral assignment to secure working capital financing. The changed text completely prohibits any assignment, including collateral assignments, which contradicts the earlier allowance as re-iterated in the contradicted text. The changed text directly conflicts with the contradicted text because the contradicted text specifically allows for a type of assignment, while the changed text forbids any assignment.
